body.gate: Defer until palace seed state verified — T-palace-as-source-of-truth is shape-locked but palace is nearly empty
body.title: Rules canonical source: DB (palace) first, CLAUDE.md becomes thin shim
body.result: theigors/rules/* seeded (11 nodes: persona, coding, commits, memory, database, budget, collaboration, igor-constraints, docs-live-in-code, do-not + parent). CLAUDE.md rewritten as ~80-line shim (bootstrap psql, destructive blocklist, env split, palace map, conflict hierarchy). /context-load Step 2a reads rules first. MEMORY.md rewritten to palace-pointer. Palace echo regen'd 1117 nodes. Migration script idempotent via UPSERT.
body.description:
Rules are currently scattered: CLAUDE.md, theigors/rules/* (palace), .claude/projects/.../memory/MEMORY.md + feedback_*.md, legacy ~/.claude/memory/*.md. Several overlap but differ. Akien 2026-04-20: pick the DB (palace) as canonical.

## Changes
- Verify theigors/rules/* is complete (coding, commits, memory, database, budget, collaboration, igor-ops, igor-constraints)
- Fill any gaps that exist only in CLAUDE.md today
- Rewrite CLAUDE.md as a thin shim: 'Read rules from SELECT ... FROM memory_palace WHERE path LIKE theigors/rules/%' + the minimum bootstrap needed to get the DB credentials and reach it
- Rules become the very first thing Claude reads on opening the DB (context-load step 2 already reads palace; make it read rules explicitly first)
- MEMORY.md entries about feedback/preferences stay where they are (those are session-persistent auto-memory, different layer)
- Add a sync check: if CLAUDE.md and theigors/rules/* disagree, palace wins and CI/audit flags the drift

## Not in scope
- Migrating tickets/decisions/slates (separate tickets)
- Auto-delete of legacy ~/.claude/memory/*.md (flag for removal, don't delete without audit)

---

## PRECONDITION FINDING 2026-04-20 (CC, during D-workflow-overhaul sprint)

The memory_palace table has only 11 rows, all under theigors/subsystem_index/*. There is NO theigors/rules/* subtree currently. MEMORY.md describes a rules-coding / rules-commits / rules-memory / rules-database / rules-budget / rules-collaboration / rules-igor-ops / rules-igor-constraints shape that does not exist in the DB. Likewise theigors/notes, theigors/igor, theigors/claude, theigors/akien subtrees claimed by MEMORY.md are absent.

Two workable sequences:
(a) First: run lab/claudecode/seed_memory_palace.py (verify current state, seed what should be there). Then audit vs CLAUDE.md for gaps. Then shrink CLAUDE.md to shim.
(b) Treat this ticket as seed+thin-shim together: port CLAUDE.md content into palace rows, delete-then-shim CLAUDE.md.

Risk on (b): if the palace read path breaks or palace_sync fails, CC loses its rules context on next session. Mitigation: keep CLAUDE.md shim with the bare-minimum rules for DB connection + verification that palace read works; degrade gracefully if palace is unreachable.

Sprint this with care — CLAUDE.md is load-bearing for every CC session start.

## ACCEPTANCE ADDITION 2026-04-20 (Akien)

The migration must preserve the ability to unfold a fresh CC on a new Linux box via the superclaude launcher. Specifically: akienasus and akienpi coming online soon. CLAUDE.md (or a functional fallback) MUST stay committed in the repo so a fresh "git clone TheIgors && superclaude" gives CC everything it needs to operate, even before the palace DB is reachable from that box. Proposed: thin shim in CLAUDE.md at repo root tries palace first, falls back to lab/recovery_help_from_akien/CLAUDE.md (regenerated by palace_sync as the always-current echo). No host-level dependencies for bootstrap.
